word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ɐ ɡˈʊd ɐkˈaʊnt ʌv wʌnsˈɛlf

senses.definition: to perform, behave, or do something extremely well, particularly in a competitive situation

examples.x: She gives a good account of herself in every job interview she attends.
They give a good account of themselves on the soccer field.
He gave a good account of himself during the important presentation.
We gave a good account of ourselves in the competition last year.
She will give a good account of herself in the upcoming exam.
They are going to give a good account of themselves in the upcoming tournament.
If he prepares well, he would give a good account of himself in the debate.
They would give a good account of themselves if given the opportunity.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp pˌʊt wˈʌnz nˈoʊz tə ðə ɡɹˈaɪndstoʊn

senses.definition: to continuously put a lot of effort into doing something

examples.x: She keeps her nose to the grindstone to meet deadlines and excel in her job.
He kept his nose to the grindstone throughout the project and delivered exceptional results.
She will put her nose to the grindstone to achieve her long-term career goals.
If he puts his nose to the grindstone, he will succeed in his endeavors.

senses.definition: to do something in a very serious and determined manner without showing mercy

examples.x: She plays for keeps in every tennis match, giving her all to win.
They play for keeps in the business world, always striving for success.
He played for keeps in the chess tournament, using advanced strategies to secure victory.
We played for keeps in the soccer championship and emerged as the champions.
She will play for keeps in the upcoming debate, determined to make a strong impression.
They are going to play for keeps in the negotiations, aiming for the best possible outcome.
If he plays for keeps, he has a chance at achieving his ambitious goals.
They would play for keeps if they were given the opportunity to compete at a higher level.

senses.definition: to be in such a bad situation that it does not matter if one fails at something, because one's situation cannot get worse

examples.x: She has nothing to lose, so she's taking a leap of faith and pursuing her dream career.
He had nothing to lose after losing everything in the fire, so he decided to travel the world and start anew.
We had nothing to lose by speaking up, so we voiced our concerns and fought for change.
She will have nothing to lose when she retires, so she plans to embark on adventurous endeavors and explore her passions.
They are going to have nothing to lose once they sell their business, allowing them to take risks in new ventures.
If he had nothing to lose, he would quit his job and pursue his true passion.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt kˈiːp sˈɛt hæv wˈʌnz ʃˈoʊldɚ tə ðə wˈiːl

senses.definition: to start something in a very energetic and determined manner

examples.x: She puts her shoulder to the wheel every day, working tirelessly to meet project deadlines.
They keep their shoulder to the wheel, collaborating closely to ensure the success of the team.
He set his shoulder to the wheel and completed the arduous task ahead of schedule.
We put our shoulder to the wheel and overcame the obstacles that stood in our way.
She will have her shoulder to the wheel and strive for excellence in her upcoming endeavors.
They are going to put their shoulder to the wheel and make significant progress in their ambitious project.
If he were to keep his shoulder to the wheel, he could achieve remarkable results.
They put their shoulders to the wheel, working together to complete the project on time.
The team keeps their shoulders to the wheel every day, striving for excellence in their work.

senses.definition: to continue operating with little ,if any, of the remaining energy, strength, or resources

examples.x: She runs on fumes, juggling multiple jobs and personal commitments.
The team is running on fumes but still giving their best effort to finish the project.
He ran on fumes for weeks during the busy season at work.
The organization ran on fumes until they secured additional funding.
They will be running on fumes if they don't take a break and recharge soon.
By the end of the week, I'll be running on fumes due to the demanding schedule.

senses.definition: to have a strong desire to achieve or do something
to do whatever one can in order to achieve what one desires

examples.x: She stops at nothing to achieve her goals, even if it means sacrificing her personal life.
The company stops at nothing to stay ahead of the competition, employing innovative strategies and relentless determination.
He stopped at nothing to win the championship, training day and night and pushing himself to the limit.
The activist group stopped at nothing to expose the truth, conducting thorough investigations and risking their safety.
They will stop at nothing to secure the deal, pursuing every opportunity and leaving no stone unturned.
The team is determined to succeed and will stop at nothing to reach their future objectives.

senses.definition: to continue trying to succeed regardless of difficulties or challenges

examples.x: I tell my friend to hang in there and keep pushing forward during their job search.
They always remind themselves to hang in there whenever they face difficult exams or assignments.
We encouraged him to hang on in there during the tough times, and eventually, he overcame the obstacles.
She hung in there and persevered through the challenging project, earning recognition for her efforts.
Tomorrow will be a challenging day, but we will hang in there and support each other until the end.
They will hang on in there and stay determined as they work towards their long-term goals.
If I were in your shoes, I would hang in there and not let setbacks discourage me.

senses.definition: to be able to handle and manage a situation or thing successfully

examples.x: She is getting on top of her workload by setting priorities and staying organized.
They get on top of their finances by creating a budget and tracking their expenses.
He got on top of the situation by addressing the issues promptly and implementing necessary changes.
We got on top of the project by working overtime and collaborating effectively.
Tomorrow, she will get on top of her studies by creating a study schedule and seeking help when needed.
They will get on top of the problem by conducting thorough research and exploring different solutions.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv wˈʌnz ɹˈaɪt ˈɑːɹm ɔːɹ ˈaɪtuːθ

senses.definition: to stop at nothing to obtain or do something

examples.x: They give their right arm to be part of the championship team.
He gives his right arm to pursue his passion for music.
I would have given my right arm to attend that concert last night.
He will give his right arm to secure a place in the prestigious university.

word_list.word: if it is the last thing {sb} [do]
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf ɪt ɪz ðə lˈæst θˈɪŋ ˌɛsbˈiː dˈuː

senses.definition: used to show how extremely determined someone is to do something
in a way that finalizes and completes the matter at hand

examples.x: I will clean my room once and for all today.
He always arrives late, so I'm confronting him once and for all.
They resolved their differences once and for all during the meeting.
We settled the matter once and for all by reaching a compromise.
I promise to finish this project once and for all by the end of the week.
They are determined to solve the issue once and for all during the next meeting.

senses.definition: the ability to hide one's emotions and seem calm in unpleasant or difficult situations

examples.x: She always keeps a stiff upper lip, no matter how challenging the situation.
They face adversity with a stiff upper lip, maintaining their composure and resolve.
He is known for his ability to maintain a stiff upper lip in difficult times.
Despite the tragedy, they managed to keep a stiff upper lip and carry on.
She held a stiff upper lip throughout the ordeal, not showing any signs of distress.
He maintained a stiff upper lip during the crisis, inspiring those around him.
Even in the face of uncertainty, she will continue to keep a stiff upper lip.
They have vowed to face any challenges that come their way with a stiff upper lip.
He plans to maintain a stiff upper lip during the upcoming difficult period.

word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ stˈɑːpɪŋ ɔːɹ hˈoʊldɪŋ ˌɛsbˈiː

senses.definition: used to say that preventing someone from doing something is not possible because of their determination
to manage to finish the most important or most difficult part of something, particularly a task

examples.x: They are determined to break the back of the project and achieve success.
She breaks the back of difficult tasks with her unwavering focus and determination.
The team breaks the back of challenges by collaborating effectively and finding innovative solutions.
He broke the back of the competition with his outstanding performance.
After months of hard work, they finally broke the back of the problem and found a solution.
The organization broke the back of financial difficulties and emerged stronger than ever.
They will break the back of the obstacle and reach their desired outcome.
With their resilience and dedication, they are destined to break the back of any challenge that comes their way.
In the future, advancements in technology will help break the back of barriers to progress.

word_list.word: like a dog with a bone
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ dˈɑːɡ wɪð ɐ bˈoʊn

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who has made their decision and is not about to let anyone or anything stop them

examples.x: She is like a dog with a bone when it comes to pursuing her career goals.
They tackle their projects with a dogged determination, always staying focused like a dog with a bone.
He pursued his passion relentlessly, holding onto it like a dog with a bone throughout his entire career.
They approached the previous project with the same determination, gripping onto it like a dog with a bone until its successful completion.
If she encounters obstacles, she will persist like a dog with a bone, never relinquishing her pursuit of success.

senses.definition: to encourage someone to start doing the most difficult or unpleasant task, rather than avoiding or postponing it

examples.x: I eat the frog by tackling the most challenging task on my to-do list first thing in the morning.
He eats that frog every day, taking on the difficult assignments head-on to increase his productivity.
She eats the frog regularly, prioritizing the toughest tasks to ensure efficient progress throughout the day.
Yesterday, I ate that frog and completed the daunting project that had been lingering for weeks.
He ate the frog last week, confronting the challenging presentation and delivering it with confidence.
She ate the frog yesterday morning, resolving the complex issue that had been causing delays.
Tomorrow, I will eat the frog by starting the day with the most difficult task on my list.
She plans to eat the frog in the coming days, tackling the challenging assignment to meet the deadline.
If I have a lot on my plate, I will eat that frog to ensure I address the most important tasks first.

senses.definition: to work as hard as one can to achieve a particular result

examples.x: He is breaking his back to meet the project deadline; he's working long hours and sacrificing his personal time.
If they want to succeed, they will have to break their back to prepare for the competition; it won't be easy.
The team is breaking their back to complete the construction project on time; they're working around the clock.
They were breaking their back to save the failing business; they put in every effort to turn it around.
By this time next week, they will be breaking their back to finish the renovations before the grand opening.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt ænd sˈoʊl ˌɪntʊ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to devote much time and effort with optimism to accomplish a goal

examples.x: She puts her heart into every project she undertakes, infusing it with passion and creativity.
I put my heart and soul into my relationships, nurturing them with love and care."
He put his heart and soul into that performance, delivering a truly captivating and emotional experience.
She put her heart into her studies, resulting in top grades and academic achievements.

senses.definition: to finish something that is left unfinished

examples.x: She ties up loose ends by organizing her schedule and completing any pending tasks.
They will tie up loose ends in the negotiation process by finalizing the contract and resolving any outstanding terms.

senses.definition: to make an attempt to do something better or faster than others

examples.x: She steps on the gas during every competition, giving her maximum effort to outperform her opponents.
They step on the gas in their business, constantly seeking innovative ways to stay ahead of the competition.
I step on the gas with my studies, dedicating extra time and effort to excel academically.
He stepped on the gas during the race and managed to secure the first-place finish.
We stepped on the gas last month to meet the project deadline, working long hours to complete it on time.
She stepped on the gas in her career, taking on new challenges and achieving remarkable professional growth.
They will step on the gas in the upcoming tournament, training rigorously to perform at their best.
I will step on the gas next week to complete the project ahead of schedule.
She plans to step on the gas in her entrepreneurial venture, investing additional resources to accelerate its growth.
If given the opportunity, they would step on the gas and seize the chance to expand their business internationally.
